human_verification.notes: Verified physician-practice-specific records, source classes, privacy and access boundaries, adjacent-lane routing, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit refusal, escalation, and physician-practice-specific evidence behavior.
Functionality mapping covers all shared abilities, all records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.

specialist_owned_evidence: The pack names physician-practice-specific artifacts including CMS-855B and CMS-855I records, PECOS and NPPES data, delegated-official attestations, records-custody addresses, privacy notices, office hours, directory records, and session-readiness artifacts.
Boundary handling is explicit for clinical scheduling approval, chart ownership, credentialing, payer enrollment, revenue activation, privacy-law interpretation, and legal or compliance signoff.
Retention, publication, access, and source-conflict questions are escalated instead of overresolved.
Runtime remains `specification-based`, with no retained-implementation claim embedded in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; state-law, source-precedence, and ownership variance are converted into required retrieval or escalation behavior.
Package is ready for validated spec-pack use and future retained-implementation promotion if repeated runtime demand justifies it.
